headline: LNG Export Revenue Falls 28% as Global Gas Prices Normalise
context: Malaysia's LNG export revenue dropped 28% to RM58 billion in 2025 from RM81 billion in 2023, as global gas prices normalised post-Ukraine crisis, squeezing Petronas margins and reducing government petroleum tax collections.

cards.big: LNG revenue down RM23 billion in 2 years — Malaysia's gas windfall from Ukraine crisis has evaporated completely
cards.sub: Petronas LNG contracts shifting from spot to long-term pricing means lower but more predictable revenue ahead

cards.big: Malaysia is world's 5th largest LNG exporter at 26 million tonnes annually — but reserves declining at 3% per year
cards.sub: At current extraction rates, proven gas reserves of 42 trillion cubic feet last only 18 years to 2043

cards.big: Sarawak's gas extraction supports 28,000 direct jobs and RM4.2 billion in local procurement spending
cards.sub: Revenue decline threatens RM800 million in planned petrochemical downstream investments in Bintulu corridor

cards.big: Petronas gas pricing agreements with Sarawak remain confidential — state cannot independently verify royalty calculations
cards.sub: Audit trail from wellhead to export terminal involves 4 Petronas subsidiaries — transfer pricing opacity is structural

cards.big: Gas reserves depleting, revenue falling, and no sovereign wealth fund to show for decades of extraction — Malaysia is spending its geological inheritance
Should Malaysia mandate that 15% of all petroleum revenue be deposited into a non-withdrawable heritage fund starting immediately?
